detectors.description:
Contract locking ether found:
	Contract MafiaDiamond (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#9-88) has payable functions:
	 - MafiaDiamond.fallback() (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#73-85)
	 - MafiaDiamond.receive() (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#87)
	But does not have a function to withdraw the ether
detectors.markdown:
Contract locking ether found:
	Contract [MafiaDiamond](contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#L9-L88) has payable functions:
	 - [MafiaDiamond.fallback()](contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#L73-L85)
	 - [MafiaDiamond.receive()](contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#L87)
	But does not have a function to withdraw the ether

detectors.description:
MafiaDiamond.constructor(address)._owner (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#33) lacks a zero-check on :
		- owner = _owner (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#34)

MafiaDiamond.transferOwnership(address).newOwner (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#66) lacks a zero-check on :
		- owner = newOwner (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#68)

MafiaDiamond.fallback() (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#73-85) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#77-84)

LibStorage.s() (contracts/libraries/LibStorage.sol#73-76) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (contracts/libraries/LibStorage.sol#75)

MafiaDiamond.removeSelectors(bytes4[]) (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#52-56) has costly operations inside a loop:
	- delete selectorToFacet[selectors[i]] (contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#54)
detectors.markdown:
[MafiaDiamond.removeSelectors(bytes4[])](contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#L52-L56) has costly operations inside a loop:
	- [delete selectorToFacet[selectors[i]]](contracts/MafiaDiamond.sol#L54)

detectors.description: Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.checkPairing() (contracts/Verifier.sol#106-171) calls Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.g1_mulAccC() (contracts/Verifier.sol#81-104) which halt the execution return(uint256,uint256)(0,0x20) (contracts/Verifier.sol#92)

Groth16Verifier.verifyProof(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],uint256[5]) (contracts/Verifier.sol#71-195) calls Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.checkPairing() (contracts/Verifier.sol#106-171) which halt the execution return(uint256,uint256)(0,0x20) (contracts/Verifier.sol#92)

Groth16Verifier.verifyProof(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],uint256[5]) (contracts/Verifier.sol#71-195) calls Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.checkField() (contracts/Verifier.sol#73-78) which halt the execution return(uint256,uint256)(0,0x20) (contracts/Verifier.sol#76)

Groth16Verifier.verifyProof(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],uint256[5]) (contracts/Verifier.sol#71-195) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (contracts/Verifier.sol#72-194)

Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.checkPairing() (contracts/Verifier.sol#106-171) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (contracts/Verifier.sol#106-171)

Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.g1_mulAccC() (contracts/Verifier.sol#81-104) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (contracts/Verifier.sol#81-104)

Groth16Verifier.verifyProof.asm_0.checkField() (contracts/Verifier.sol#73-78) uses assembly
	- INLINE ASM (contracts/Verifier.sol#73-78)

Version constraint >=0.7.0<0.9.0 is too complex.
It is used by:
	- >=0.7.0<0.9.0 (contracts/Verifier.sol#21)
